summary: Learner treats 1 as a prime number.
watchFor: Learner uses memorized labels without divisor reasoning.
Learner says 1 is prime because it has no pairs other than 1.
operatorNote: Bring the learner back to the definition of prime as exactly two divisors.

summary: Learner omits repeated prime factors when writing prime factorized form.
watchFor: Learner rewrites 12 as 2 x 3 instead of 2 x 2 x 3.
Learner can split correctly in scratch work but compresses too much in the final line.
operatorNote: Ask the learner to rebuild the final line from scratch work without dropping repeated factors.

summary: Learner accepts a factorization because the product matches, without checking whether all factors are prime.
watchFor: Learner approves 2 x 6 for 12 because it multiplies correctly.
Learner never checks the meaning of the word 'prime' in prime factorization.
operatorNote: Freeze the product check and inspect whether every factor is prime first.

summary: Learner lists divisors one by one instead of reusing the prime factorization structure.
watchFor: Learner restarts from 1, 2, 3, ... despite already having a factorization.
Learner cannot say what information the factorization is supposed to provide.
operatorNote: Ask what the completed factorization already gives before any divisor list starts.

summary: Learner chooses a random multiplier for a square-number task instead of using the missing prime-factor counts.
watchFor: Learner guesses a multiplier and only checks afterward.
Learner does not inspect which prime count is odd.
operatorNote: Have the learner mark odd prime counts before proposing any missing factor.

summary: Learner mixes common-divisor and common-multiple reasoning when comparing two factorizations.
watchFor: Learner uses overlap language and cover language interchangeably.
Learner starts multiplying without classifying the task first.
Learner can repeat the words GCD or LCM but cannot connect them to shared-versus-covering structure.
operatorNote: Pause calculation and make the learner say whether the task asks for overlap or coverage before any arithmetic begins.

summary: Learner cannot explain which part of the factorization matters for the new question.
watchFor: Learner recites the whole factorization without selecting a useful part.
Learner solves procedurally and cannot justify why that part was used.
Learner points to a factor or exponent but cannot say how it helps answer the new question.
operatorNote: Require the learner to point to the relevant factor or exponent before computing and explain why that part matters.
